zotero.abstractNote: Vision-Language Models (VLMs) have demonstrated remarkable capabilities in reasoning about visual content. However, it remains unclear whether these models truly understand the underlying data-types of the visual information they process. In this work, we investigate whether VLMs can identify the data-type of a visual input (e.g., whether an image is a scatter plot, a heatmap, or a natural photograph). We find that despite their impressive performance on general visual tasks, VLMs struggle to identify the data-type of visual inputs. Furthermore, we show that this limitation does not improve with scaling, suggesting that data-type understanding is not an emergent property of current VLM training paradigms. We introduce a new benchmark, SyntheticTypeIdent, to evaluate this capability and demonstrate that even state-of-the-art models perform poorly on this task.
zotero.proceedingsTitle: The Twelfth International Conference on Learning Representations
